title: lobelia gregoriana subsp. elgonensis
intro: Lobelia gregoriana subsp. elgonensis, also known as Elgon lobelia, is a plant species within the Campanulaceae family. It is native to East Africa and has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antispasmodic effects. The health benefits of Lobelia gregoriana subsp. elgonensis include its ability to relieve respiratory issues, such as bronchitis and asthma, as well as its potential to reduce stress and anxiety. In herbal preparations, the plant is often made into teas, infusions, and tinctures, and can be used to treat various ailments due to its adaptogenic and expectorant properties.
